name: REVERSE rapamycin and ProLon registry
summary: Active-not-recruiting randomized open-label early phase 1 prevention pilot testing weekly rapamycin, fasting-mimicking diet, and combination context against methylation-age, cognition, insulin-resistance, and safety endpoints.
population: Healthy real-world cohort aged 50 to 80 years with stable conditions allowed if they do not conflict with rapamycin

trial_details.expected_results_window: The registry listed estimated primary completion in February 2026 and estimated study completion in May 2026, with no posted results in the June 11, 2026 API view.
trial_details.horizon_note: Active-not-recruiting no-results registry context; the combination with fasting-mimicking diet limits attribution to rapamycin alone.
trial_details.why_it_matters: This trial is directly relevant to Rapalogs because it tests weekly rapamycin in an aging-labeled prevention study, but it should remain no-results watch context until methylation-age, functional, and safety data are posted or published.
